name: Grade 2 Language Arts — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 2 pathway for paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 2 knowledge and transferable skills in paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 2 task connected to paragraphs, narrative, informational, and opinion writing.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
